environment.androidAvd: Pixel_8_2 API 34 arm64, Android 14, 1080x2400 @420dpi; headless boot succeeded

results.details: The packed tarball resolved from an isolated consumer root and bundled the full matrix fixture with plugins for both Metro platforms.

Packed Expo 54 / React Native 0.81 applications built in Release, installed, launched, and visibly rendered the deterministic fixture on the designated iOS and Android simulators; Android also passed native hierarchy assertions.
Expo 56 / React Native 0.85 packed-consumer bundling passed from an isolated root under the release-proven ^0.81.0 || ^0.85.0 peer range.
Packed Expo 56 / React Native 0.85 applications built in Release, installed, launched, and visibly rendered the fixture on the designated iOS and Android simulators.

Real Shiki JavaScript-regex engine, RaTeX, beautiful-mermaid/react-native-svg, and react-native-webview adapters resolved and bundled on both Metro platforms. No runtime render or resource-budget claim is made.

A fresh packed Expo 56 / React Native 0.85 app auto-linked and rendered real Shiki JavaScript-regex, RaTeX, beautiful-mermaid/react-native-svg, and react-native-webview providers in Release-Hermes. Provider-completion semantics passed on Android UIAutomator and iOS Maestro, fatal logs were empty, and both captures were human reviewed. The run found and fixed beautiful-mermaid browser-CSS incompatibility at the strict native SVG boundary. Physical-device resource budgets remain unclaimed.

The packed Expo 56 Release-Hermes fixture passed 24 reviewed cases per platform with semantic assertions before every capture. Human review caught and corrected fullscreen safe-area overlap. Final clean compares passed at 0.007%-0.046% on iOS and 0.079%-0.130% on Android, below the pinned 0.250% budget.

blockers: No physical Pixel 8 or iPhone 15 reference device is connected for release-Hermes frame, heap, or startup profiling.
Manual VoiceOver and TalkBack hardware verification has not been performed.

The Android emulator streaming characterization exceeded the reference-device p95 budget and is recorded as non-reference evidence, not a pass. Optional-provider startup, heap-growth, long-block, and frame budgets are included in the same physical reference-device gate.
